vision.title: Kube9 VS Code: Kubernetes management made simple inside the IDE
vision.mission: Help developers and platform engineers operate Kubernetes safely and quickly from VS Code by combining visual cluster workflows, GitOps visibility, and optional operator-powered intelligence.
vision.core_purpose: Kube9 VS Code exists to remove Kubernetes context switching and CLI friction. It provides an IDE-native control plane for everyday cluster tasks (navigation, inspect, edit, apply, logs, events, port-forwarding, and ArgoCD actions) while preserving user control through local-first operation and kubectl-native access.
vision.key_architecture: Dual-mode product model: (1) strong standalone extension value with local webviews and direct kubectl/API interactions using kubeconfig, and (2) progressive enhancement when kube9-operator is present, unlocking operated dashboards, richer status, and AI recommendation surfaces tied to free/pro operator tiers.

strategic_goals.now: Win as a trusted open-source VS Code extension for daily Kubernetes operations: reliable multi-cluster workflows, fast resource interactions, strong onboarding/tutorials, and practical GitOps visibility through ArgoCD integration.
strategic_goals.next: Make operated mode the default path for teams that need deeper insights: clearer free-vs-pro value boundaries, better recommendation quality, and tighter day-2 workflows that connect troubleshooting, events, and remediation actions.
strategic_goals.later: Become the developer-facing front door of the broader Kube9 ecosystem across interfaces and environments, with proactive intelligence and policy-aware guidance that helps teams prevent incidents rather than react to them.

key_principles: Developer-first workflow: every feature must reduce context switching and operational cognitive load.
Simple by default, powerful when needed: core workflows must be immediately useful before advanced capabilities are introduced.
Progressive enhancement: free/standalone experience is complete; operator-powered and pro capabilities add depth without breaking baseline value.
Local-first trust model: kubeconfig and operational control remain with the user; avoid unnecessary external dependencies for core tasks.
Actionable intelligence over noisy telemetry: insights should prioritize clear severity, direct remediation, and measurable user impact.
GitOps-aware operations: support modern deployment workflows (ArgoCD) as a first-class part of cluster management.

competitive_advantages: IDE-native Kubernetes management that fits existing developer flow instead of requiring a separate dashboard app.
Broad practical command surface in one extension (resource CRUD, YAML, logs, events, port forwarding, context switching, Helm, ArgoCD actions).
Clear progression from local standalone usage to operator-enhanced intelligence with free/pro tiers.
Cross-cluster, kubeconfig-native compatibility without forcing vendor lock-in to a specific managed Kubernetes provider.
Strong onboarding and discoverability via tutorial, command palette, and contextual help paths.
differentiation_from_competitors: vs kubectl-only workflows: Kube9 keeps kubectl compatibility but adds visual speed, discoverability, and guided actions.
vs standalone Kubernetes UIs: Kube9 emphasizes in-IDE continuity, reducing tool-hopping during development and debugging.
vs generic cluster viewers: Kube9 combines day-1 operations and day-2 signals (events, operated dashboards, ArgoCD drift context) in one developer workspace.
vs cloud-specific consoles: Kube9 is cluster-agnostic and portable across any environment reachable through kubeconfig.
vs pure AI assistants: Kube9 is execution-integrated product UX, not only advice; actions and context live where operators already work.
